claim: nikdanilov/whisper-obsidian-plugin is the most-starred dedicated Whisper plugin for Obsidian at 350 stars, latest release v1.9.1 in April 2026, supporting OpenAI/Groq/Azure/Ollama/LM Studio backends, hotkey recording (Alt+Q), LLM post-processing, and URL automation for iOS Shortcuts.
quote: record or upload audio, transcribe with Whisper, and optionally post-process the result with an LLM ... Works with multiple Whisper-compatible APIs including OpenAI, Groq, Azure, and self-hosted options like Ollama and LM Studio.

claim: djmango/obsidian-transcription has 222 stars and supports both local Whisper ASR and cloud transcription with ffmpeg-based audio handling and timestamp markers, but its last release (v3.4.0) dates from April 2024 — clearly less actively maintained than nikdanilov in 2026.
quote: Whisper ASR (local, open-source) ... OpenAI Whisper integration ... Latest Release: Version 3.4.0 (April 12, 2024) ... 222 stars

claim: Smart Memos (Mossy1022, 79 stars, last release Sept 2024) is OpenAI-API-only with customizable LLM prompts for transcript+summary+expansion in one pass, with local-model support still on the roadmap.
quote: This plugin currently uses online openAI models to recieve and transcribe your voice memos ... Local Model Transcription is listed as a high priority

claim: Hands-on testing of nikdanilov's plugin shows OpenAI Whisper API costs ~$1 per 2h45m of audio with strong accent handling, while local whisper.cpp gives reduced accuracy, occasional accent failures, and needs ~16GB RAM and meaningful CPU.
quote: Costs approximately $1 per 2 hours 45 minutes of audio ... Reduced accuracy compared to the API version ... Occasional difficulty recognizing accents ... Requires approximately 16GB RAM

claim: Whisper Large-v3 hits 2.7% WER on clean English but degrades to 8–12% WER on real-world English meetings/podcasts, and accented English (Indian, Scottish) lands at 8–15% WER — a 5–10 percentage point penalty over standard.
quote: 2.7% WER on LibriSpeech test-clean ... 8–12% WER on real-world English ... Accented English (Indian, Scottish) ... 8–15% WER depending on accent strength

claim: The 2026 Obsidian forum consensus on voice-to-Obsidian is a four-stage pipeline (voice memo → transcription → AI summary → structured notes → vault), with friction concentrated around fragmented recordings from interruptions rather than transcription quality.
quote: Voice memo → transcription → AI summary → structured notes → Obsidian ... Interruptions (phone calls, Bluetooth switching) fragment single thoughts into multiple recordings

claim: On iPhone, the dominant DIY pattern is iOS Shortcuts plus RoutineHub-shared shortcuts that pipe Voice Memos through Whisper (cloud or local via Aiko) and ChatGPT formatting, then drop output into iCloud where the Obsidian vault picks it up.
quote: Uses 'openai whisper' for transcription and ChatGPT for formatting, then saves files to iCloud ... Aiko runs 'whisper entirely offline on my iphone so that the data doesn't leave my device' ... iOS Shortcuts: Apple's built-in automation platform

claim: Apple added on-device transcription to Voice Memos in iOS 18, processing locally on iPhone 12+, achieving roughly 85–90% accuracy on clear single-speaker audio but degrading with multiple speakers, length, or formatting needs.
quote: Starting with iOS 18, Apple added automatic transcription to the Voice Memos app ... Apple tries to process transcripts directly on your device ... 85-90% accuracy on clear, single-speaker recordings

claim: The AudioPen→Obsidian bridge (jonashaefele, 7 stars, last release Dec 2024) uses AudioPen webhooks with templated variables ({title},{body},{orig_transcript}) — a niche but established pattern for users who already pay for AudioPen's polishing engine.
quote: synchronizes audio transcriptions between AudioPen (or VoiceNotes) and Obsidian ... Users configure webhook URLs in AudioPen's integration settings ... Version 0.2.0 was released on December 23, 2024

claim: Vox (vincentbavitz, 78 stars) auto-watches an unprocessed folder and transcribes via the developer's free remote server (100 transcriptions/day), with filename-prefix tokens for importance/category metadata — a hands-off alternative for users who don't want to manage API keys.
quote: monitors a designated 'unprocessed' folder, transcribes audio files, and saves the transcribed text with metadata and tags ... 100 transcriptions/day limit per vault ... Files are only held in memory as buffers and are not saved to disk

claim: Wispr Flow positions itself as a system-wide voice keyboard for Obsidian claiming 220 wpm vs 45 wpm typing (~4x), cross-platform sync across Mac/Windows/iOS/Android, and natural-style adaptation rather than literal dictation.
quote: 4x faster than typing (220 words per minute vs. 45 wpm for keyboard input) ... Cross-platform: Available on Mac, Windows, iPhone, and Android with synced settings
